item.title: US Sanctions Key Cuban Bank and Miner in Fresh Blow to Havana
item.description: The US sanctioned the Cuban military’s international banking arm and a state-run company that’s partnered with Trafigura Group and other miners, in the latest escalation of pressure against Havana’s communist leadership.

item.title: Massachusetts Court Kills Proposal for Toughest US Rent Control
item.description: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court struck down a ballot proposal that would have imposed the strictest statewide rent control in the US.

item.title: US Natural Gas Falls on Cooler Outlooks, Smaller LNG Flows
item.description: US natural gas futures declined as forecasts shifted to show cooler weather in the coming weeks, suggesting a slight decline in demand for gas-fired electricity as consumers pull back on using air-conditioners. US gas flows to liquefied natural gas export facilities also fell, increasing supply in the domestic market.

item.title: Russia’s Gasoline Crunch Spans Country After Refinery Attacks
item.description: Russia’s gasoline crunch is worsening, with at least two-thirds of the country’s regions introducing fuel rationing or suffering supply disruptions following relentless Ukrainian drone attacks on refineries.

item.title: Supreme Court Puts Curbs on Human-Rights Suits in Cisco Win
item.description: A divided US Supreme Court put new limits on lawsuits against companies over atrocities abroad, rejecting a suit accusing Cisco Systems Inc. of complicity in China’s torture of Falun Gong adherents.

item.title: RBI Allows Banks to Lend Against Forex Deposits to Boost Inflows
item.description: India’s central bank allowed lenders to extend loans against foreign-currency deposits raised under its special program aimed at attracting overseas funds, a move that could increase participation in the plan and boost dollar inflows.

item.title: Iran and Oman Say They’ll Work on Pact for Hormuz Transit Costs
item.description: Iran and Oman said they’ll begin work on finding an agreement over the future administration of the Strait of Hormuz, including the cost of managing transit, as traders and shipowners fret over the prospect of the introduction of a tolling arrangement.

item.title: Texas Power Trader e360 Alters Course After Shutting Hedge Fund
item.description: e360 Power is pursuing a more conservative trading strategy after a period of poor performance caused the firm that specializes in US natural gas and power markets to close its flagship hedge fund.

item.title: US Business Activity Picks Up on Manufacturing Strength
item.description: US business activity expanded at the fastest pace in five months, bolstered by a surge in demand for manufactured goods.

item.title: Tonino Lamborghini Plans Luxury Hotel at South Africa’s Zimbali
item.description: Tonino Lamborghini is partnering with Zimbali Lakes Resort to develop a luxury hotel and branded residences near South Africa’s port city of Durban.

item.title: Evercore’s Emanuel Says Earnings Will Rescue Tech From Selloff
item.description: Megacap technology stocks will return to investor favor following a selloff that has dragged some of the sector’s biggest names to losses of more than 10% in recent weeks, Evercore ISI’s Julian Emanuel said.

item.title: Macklem Warns Over-Investment in US Poses Financial Risks
item.description: The Bank of Canada’s top official is warning that over-investment in the US is creating broader correction risks for the global financial system.

item.title: Morocco Takes Rate Pause Into Second Year With Eye on Iran Talks
item.description: Morocco kept interest rates on hold, seeking to keep borrowing flowing for a football World Cup-linked building boom while guarding against inflationary risks as the US and Iran try and reach a lasting peace deal.

item.title: Russian Oil Exports Hit 2026 High as Iran Waiver Threatens Sales
item.description: Tumbling oil prices after the interim US-Iran peace deal are eating into Moscow’s windfall

item.title: Stada Weighs €6 Billion Takeover of CVC-Backed Cooper Consumer Health
item.description: Stada is exploring a potential purchase of Cooper Consumer Health from its private equity owners, people familiar with the matter said, as it hunts for acquisitions on the back of record profits.

item.title: Crypto Critic Nouriel Roubini Finds a Use for the Blockchain
item.description: Nouriel Roubini, the economist known for predicting the 2008 financial crisis and later for his sharp criticism of crypto, is putting one of his investment products on the blockchain.

item.title: Meta Debuts Glasses Under Its Own Brand at Lower $299 Price
item.description: The company is also considering camera-free models in the future.

item.title: Early Anthropic Backer Menlo Lands $3 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Haul
item.description: In 2024, Menlo Ventures made the risky decision to raise $500 million to invest in Anthropic PBC, then an underdog rival to OpenAI that had yet to generate meaningful revenue or gain mainstream adoption like ChatGPT.

item.title: Europe Has $200 Billion Treasury Leverage Over US, Study Argues
item.description: Europe’s outsized holdings of US Treasuries give it significant sway in any future standoff with President Donald Trump, according to new research.

item.title: Platinum Is Said Sole Remaining Bidder for Nestle Water Unit
item.description: Platinum Equity is the last remaining bidder for Nestle SA’s water business after rival Clayton Dubilier & Rice dropped out of the race, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: North Sea Crude Weakens as Mideast Oil Floods Back to Market
item.description: Key indicators in the North Sea crude market are showing signs of weakness as a wave of Middle Eastern oil heads to Europe, fueling concerns about a supply glut following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Says Easing Hinges on New Data as Risks Rise
item.description: Brazil’s central bank said adjustments to its easing cycle will be made based on new information after policymakers mulled different paths for the key rate and affirmed that inflation risks are skewed to the upside.

item.title: ‘Looking Over Our Shoulder:’ Traders Are Loading Up on VIX Calls
item.description: Summer is officially here for US stock traders. Recession odds aren’t alarming. The US and Iran are negotiating. Corporate America’s profits are at record highs. And trade policy? Not too much of a worry — at least for now.

item.title: Hungary Cuts Key Rate After Forint Gain Slows Inflation
item.description: Hungary’s central bank resumed cutting one of the highest key interest rates in the European Union, bucking a global tilt toward monetary tightening after the forint’s strengthening slowed inflation.

item.title: Iran’s Race to Secure New Oil Buyers Won’t Be Easy
item.description: Tehran has just a couple of months to offer its crude far and wide, and the market dynamics aren’t in its favor.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 23, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 1.3% as of 7:41 a.m. in New York amid a global selloff in technology stocks.

item.title: Tesla Stockholders Bet on SpaceX Merger as Musk’s Real Endgame
item.description: It’s been a challenging year for Tesla Inc. shareholders, losing almost 10% while watching Elon Musk’s SpaceX complete the biggest initial public offering ever and immediately become the most valuable entity in the newly minted trillionaire’s collection.

item.title: Emerging Market Stocks Fall on Korea Selloff; Currencies Weaken
item.description: A selloff in South Korea’s technology giants dragged the gauge of emerging-market equities down, while most developing-nations’ currencies weakened as global risk sentiment worsened.

item.title: Games Industry Faces More Painful Months Ahead
item.description: The world’s biggest games publisher is discussing axing some investments

item.title: Zerodha’s Kamath Sees Energy Transition Stocks as Key India Bet
item.description: Energy transition has become a key investment theme for Nikhil Kamath, co-founder of Zerodha Broking Ltd., as the US-Iran war reinforces the sector’s importance and creates opportunities across its value chain.
